originalTitle: Millions listen to Ethiopian star's song taking swipe at government
title: Ethiopia's Star Sings About Friendship!
content:
Hello from Ethiopia!

Hello, my little friends! I am your news reporter from Africa. Today, we have a very special story from a country called Ethiopia. Ethiopia is a beautiful country with many amazing people. It is a place where many exciting things happen! We are going to talk about a very famous singer.

Meet Teddy Afro!

In Ethiopia, there is a very big star. His name is Teddy Afro. He is a super popular singer! Everyone in Ethiopia knows Teddy Afro. People love to listen to his songs. His music makes people happy. Some of his songs make people want to dance. He sings with a warm and lovely voice. He is like a music superhero for many people there.

What is his new song about?

Teddy Afro has a brand new song out now. Many, many people are listening to it. This song is very special. It talks about something important. Teddy Afro sings about wanting everyone in his country to be good friends. He wants everyone to work together and help each other. He sings about being "together."

What does "Unity" mean?

The special word for everyone being together and helping each other is "unity." Can you say "unity"? It means being friends and sticking together. Imagine you are playing a game with your friends. If you all play together and help each other, you are showing unity. If one friend falls, and another helps them up, that is unity too. It means being one big, happy team.

Why is it good to be united?

When people in a country have unity, many good things happen. They can build better schools. They can make sure everyone has yummy food. They can help sick people get better. When everyone is friends and helps each other, the country becomes strong. It's like when all the little ants work together to carry a big leaf. They are united, and they can do big things! Teddy Afro wants this for his home.

Music helps us feel!

Music is amazing, isn't it? Songs can make us feel happy or even a little bit sad. They can make us want to dance and sing along. Teddy Afro's new song makes people think. It makes them think about being kind to each other. It makes them think about helping their neighbours. Music can tell important stories and share big ideas. It's a fun way to learn and feel.

Did you know about Ethiopia?

Did you know that Ethiopia is a very old country? It has a very long history! Did you know that Ethiopia is famous for coffee? Yes, the yummy drink many grown-ups like comes from there! Did you know that the capital city of Ethiopia is called Addis Ababa? It's a big, busy city! Did you know that Ethiopia has many different kinds of music? Some use drums, some use special guitars. Did you know that the official language in Ethiopia is Amharic? But many people speak other languages too! These facts show how special Ethiopia is. Teddy Afro is singing about this special place.

Many friends are listening!

Lots and lots of people in Ethiopia are listening to Teddy Afro's new song. Millions of people! That's like all the kids in many, many schools listening to one song! They are listening on their phones. They are listening on the radio. They are listening in their homes. They are talking about the song. They like its message of friendship and working together. The song is traveling all over the country.

Teddy Afro's Big Dream

Teddy Afro has a big dream for his country. He dreams that all the people in Ethiopia will live together peacefully. He wants them to share and care. He wants them to remember they are all part of one big family, the family of Ethiopia. His song helps share this big, kind dream with everyone. He wants a happy future for all children in Ethiopia. He wants them to grow up in a place where everyone is a friend.

Let's all be friends!

So, Teddy Afro's song is a message of love and friendship. It's about bringing people together. It reminds us that being kind to each other is very important. No matter where we live, we can all learn from Teddy Afro's song. We can try to be more united with our friends and our family. We can help each other every day. Let's sing a song for friendship too!
summary: Ethiopian music star Teddy Afro has released a new song that many people are listening to, and it talks about how important it is for everyone in his country to be friends and work together.
whyMatters: This is important because when famous people sing about being kind and united, it helps many children and grown-ups remember to be good friends and work together to make their country a happy place for everyone.

originalTitle: ‘No regrets’: Venezuela’s Machado defends giving Nobel medal to Trump
title: A Special Prize for Helping Peace!
content:
Jambo, my amazing young friends! It's your favorite kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share a super interesting story from far, far away. Today, we're talking about a very special award and a big 'thank you' gift!

What is a Nobel Peace Prize?

Imagine getting a shiny medal and a big certificate for doing something really, really good. Not just good for your family, but good for the whole world! That's a bit like the Nobel Peace Prize. It's one of the most famous and important awards ever! People who get this prize are special heroes. They work hard to make sure everyone can live together happily, without fighting. They help solve problems with talking, not with anger. They are champions of peace! It's like when you share your toys with a friend, or help someone who is sad. You are making peace! The Nobel Peace Prize is for grown-ups who do this on a very, very big scale. Did you know? The Nobel Peace Prize is named after a smart man called Alfred Nobel. He invented many things, but he wanted to be remembered for peace, so he started these prizes to honor people who do good.

Meet Maria Corina Machado

Now, let's meet a very brave lady. Her name is Maria Corina Machado. Can you say that with me? Mah-CHAH-doh. She comes from a country far away called Venezuela. It's in South America, a beautiful place with big mountains and lovely beaches. Maria Corina Machado is a leader in her country. She cares a lot about the people of Venezuela. She wants them to live in a happy, fair place where everyone has what they need and can speak freely. She works very hard to make her country better for everyone. Sometimes, leaders in a country face big challenges. Maria Corina Machado has been working for a long time to help her country when things were not going so well. She is like a super-hero for her people, always trying to make a difference and bring good changes.

Meet Donald Trump

Next, we have Mr. Donald Trump. He used to be the President of a very big country called America. It's also known as the United States. Being a president is a very important job, like being the head of a huge family! Presidents make big decisions that affect many, many people. They talk to leaders from other countries and try to help make the world a safer place. Mr. Trump was doing this important job for some years. Did you know? America is a huge country! It's so big that if you drove across it, it would take many, many days. It has deserts, tall mountains, and even very cold places!

About Nicolas Maduro

In Maria Corina Machado's country, Venezuela, there was another leader named Nicolas Maduro. He was the president there. But not everyone was happy with how things were going in Venezuela when he was leading. Many people, including Maria Corina Machado, felt that the country needed big changes. They wanted things to be different and better for the families living there. They wanted more freedom and a better life for everyone. Sometimes, when leaders don't listen to their people, things can get very hard. This was happening in Venezuela, and many people were hoping for someone to help make a change and bring peace back.

A Special Gift and Why

So, here's the exciting part! Maria Corina Machado had a Nobel Peace Prize. Yes, one of those super special awards for making peace in the world! But she didn't keep it for herself. She decided to give her Nobel Peace Prize to Mr. Donald Trump! Isn't that amazing? Giving away something so precious! Why did she do this? She said she gave it to him because she felt he helped her country, Venezuela. She believed that Mr. Trump, as the President of America, did something to help change things with Nicolas Maduro. For a Class 2 student, 'helped' in this story means he helped to make a very important change or helped to stop a difficult situation. It doesn't mean like catching someone in a game of tag! It means helping to fix a big problem that was making people sad. Maria Corina Machado saw Mr. Trump's actions as a way of helping her people in Venezuela get closer to the peace and freedom they wished for. It was her big way of saying 'THANK YOU'! Did you know? Saying 'thank you' in different languages is fun! In Swahili, we say 'Asante.' In Afrikaans, it's 'Dankie'! In Spanish, it's 'Gracias'!

What This Story Means

This story tells us something very important. It tells us that people everywhere want peace. They want to live in countries where leaders listen and help make things good for everyone. It also shows us that a 'thank you' can be a very big thing! Maria Corina Machado chose to give the highest honor she had to show her gratitude. That's a powerful message! Even though countries are far apart, what happens in one place can affect others. It's like we are all part of one big global family, and we all want each other to be happy and peaceful. This gift was a symbol. It was like saying, 'Thank you for helping us stand up for what is right and for peace.'

Your Part in Making Peace!

So, what can you, my little superheroes, learn from this? You can learn that helping others is one of the best things you can do. Whether it's helping your friend with homework, sharing your snack, or just being kind to everyone. Every little act of kindness and peace you do makes the world a better place. You might not get a Nobel Peace Prize today, but you are making peace in your own schools and homes! Remember to be kind, listen to your teachers and parents, and always try to help those around you. That's how we all build a better world, piece by piece, just like Maria Corina Machado hopes for her country. And that's our special news story for today! Keep being curious, keep learning, and keep spreading kindness wherever you go. Until next time, this is your African kid-friendly reporter signing off! Kwaheri!
summary: Maria Corina Machado gave her special Nobel Peace Prize to former President Donald Trump because she felt he helped her country, Venezuela, by making a big change with its leader, Nicolas Maduro.
whyMatters: This story shows us how much people want peace and good leaders, and that a big 'thank you' can be given in very special ways when someone helps make a difficult situation better for many people. It reminds us that helping others is always important for a better world.

originalTitle: “Lords of war.” Brazil’s president condemns UN Security Council
title: Leaders Talk About Making Our World Peaceful!
content:
Hello, my amazing friends across Africa! It's your friendly kid reporter here, bringing you super important news from our big, beautiful world. Today, we're going to talk about something grown-ups call "peace" and how big countries try to work together so everyone can live happily and safely.

A Big Meeting Place for Countries

Imagine your school has a big assembly hall. All the classes come together there to talk about important things, right? Well, the world has a super-duper big assembly hall for all the countries! It’s called the **United Nations**, or the UN for short. The UN is like a club for almost all the countries on Earth. They meet to talk about how to make our world a better place. They want to stop big fights, help people who are hungry, and make sure children can go to school and stay healthy. It’s all about making sure everyone is safe and happy. Did you know? Many, many countries right here in Africa are part of the United Nations! They send their leaders to these big meetings to share ideas and help make important decisions for our continent and the whole world.

A Special Team for Peace

Inside this big UN club, there’s a very special team. It's like the main prefects or the peacekeepers of the whole school. This team is called the **UN Security Council**. Their job is super, super important! Their main goal is to make sure there are no big fights between countries and that everyone stays safe. They are like the world's firefighters, rushing to put out arguments before they become big fires. This special team has 15 members, like 15 chairs around a big table. But here’s something interesting: five of these chairs are always taken by the same five countries. They are called the "permanent members." Did you know? The UN Security Council was created a very long time ago after a super big world war, to try and stop such wars from happening again.

The Five Big Leaders

So, who are these five countries that always have a seat at the main table? They are the United States of America, the United Kingdom, France, China, and Russia. These countries are very powerful. They have big armies and lots of important things. Because they are so powerful, they have a very special "superpower" at the Security Council. It's called a **veto**. Imagine if your friends are voting on what game to play, and one friend can say "No!" and stop the game from happening, even if everyone else said "Yes." That's a bit like what the veto power is! If one of these five big countries says "No" to an idea, that idea cannot happen, even if the other 14 countries agree. This superpower is meant to make sure big decisions are very carefully thought about. But sometimes, it can make it hard for the UN to do its job, especially when these five big countries don't agree. Did you know? These five permanent members were the main winning countries from that big world war a long time ago. That’s why they got these special seats!

A Leader From Brazil Speaks Up!

Now, let's talk about some news! There's a country far, far away in South America called **Brazil**. It's a very big country with beautiful rainforests and amazing animals. The leader of Brazil, who is like the president, recently said something important about these five big countries. He looked at these five powerful countries in the UN Security Council and called them "Lords of War." What does "Lords of War" mean? Well, it means he felt that sometimes, these powerful countries act in ways that are more about fighting or causing trouble, instead of always trying to keep peace. He wasn't saying they *want* war, but he felt that because they have so much power, and sometimes don't agree, they might accidentally make it harder to find peaceful solutions. Or perhaps, because they make and sell many weapons, it felt to him like they were more involved in fighting than in helping stop fights. It’s like when some big kids in the playground have all the best toys and decide all the games, but sometimes their decisions cause more arguments instead of everyone having fun.

Why Did He Say That?

The president of Brazil said this because he wants things to be fair. He believes that the UN Security Council needs to change. He thinks it's not right that only five countries have so much power with their "veto" button. He wants more countries from all over the world, including more from Africa and South America, to have a stronger say in making peace decisions. He wants everyone to work together, so that peace is truly for everyone, not just decided by a few. He believes that if more voices are heard, and more countries have a chance to help, the world will be a much safer and more peaceful place for all children. Did you know? Many countries, not just Brazil, think the UN Security Council should have more members from different parts of the world to make it more fair and strong.

Why Is This Important for Us?

You might be thinking, "Why does this matter to me, a kid in Africa?" Well, my dear friends, it matters a lot! When big countries argue, or when they don't agree on how to keep peace, it can affect everyone. Sometimes, disagreements in one part of the world can make problems in another part. For us in Africa, we want a world where there are no big wars, where children can go to school without fear, and where families can grow food and live happily. When leaders like Brazil’s president speak up, they are trying to make the world better for all of us. They are saying, "Let's all work together fairly to keep peace!" And that is something truly important for every child, every family, and every country. Let's all hope that leaders keep talking and working hard to build a peaceful world for everyone. And remember, even you can help by being kind to your friends and sharing your toys!
summary: A leader from Brazil thinks that the most powerful countries in a special world team should work more fairly to make sure there is peace for everyone.
whyMatters: It's important because when big countries work together fairly for peace, everyone in the world, including children in Africa, can live safely and happily without worries about fighting.
